Yields of products from thermal neutron-induced
'OSIRIS' was used for rapid separation of the fission
products. The earlier experiments were made with a
target/ion source temperature of about 1500 deg C,
and the results are designated either 'B' (dating
from 1980) or 'C' (dating from 1985) in the main refe-
rence. The more recent experiments were performed
with a hotter target/ion source of 2400 deg C and
a modified experimental technique; these are results
'A' in the main ref and date from 1986.
points have been used for each element in order to
determine the element dependent delay properties
inherent in the ISOL method, see REL-REF.
The yields chosen are flagged in the main data table.

The results referred to as 'A' in the main reference
have not been previously reported. Those referred to
as 'B' appeared in EXFOR 22003 (now superseded by this
entry) whereas results 'C' were not previously
available since they appeared only in an unpublished
reference. In some cases the 'B' and 'C' results have
been recalculated using new normalization yields or
using the hotter (2400 deg C.) Target/ion source,
